category_title: At-risk four-year-old students exhibiting varied oral language behaviors
minimal_context: The teacher is leading a storybook reading activity during circle time to help the student develop language skills.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We are reading the storybook now. What is the animal on this page?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: That is correct. What is the dog doing in the picture?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: No, that is incorrect. He is jumping. Where is he jumping?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks down, hesitant to use more words) Bed.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We are reading the storybook now. What is the animal on this page?

STUDENT: Dog.

TUTOR: That is correct. What is the dog doing in the picture?

STUDENT: Run.

TUTOR: No, that is incorrect. He is jumping. Where is he jumping?

STUDENT: (Looks down, hesitant to use more words) Bed.

task.content: Participating in teacher-directed experiences where complex language is required
task.learning_goal: Develop the language and literacy skills necessary for school success

context.social_setting: Teacher-directed experience (e.g., circle time, storybook reading, and meal time)

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When adults were asking predominantly low level questions or indicating whether a particular answer was correct or incorrect
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Employed more undesired language usage, used one word responses or phrases
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Hesitant to use more elaborate language due to the fear of making grammatical errors

behavior_patterns.condition: When speaking with either their teachers or paraprofessionals during teaching directed experiences
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Engaged in desired language usage when speaking to adults less than one-third of the time
